title: Puffed Korean Peppers Wrap
description: A light and flavorful Korean-inspired dish featuring sweet red peppers, crispy lamination-puff, and served in a ti-leaf wrap. Perfect for a brunch gathering.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add the Korean chili flakes and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the gochujang and soy sauce, and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Roll out the lamination-puff pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut the pastry into four squares, each about 6 inches per side.
Brush the pastry squares with sesame oil and top with a Korean red pepper, a sprinkle of black sesame seeds, and a sprinkle of chopped green onions.
Fold the pastry in half to enclose the filling, and press the edges to seal.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a ti-leaf wrap.
Place a pastry-wrapped pepper in the center of the wrap and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the pastry is golden brown.
Flip the wrap over and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the other side is also golden brown.
